project.name: CodeLens
project.tagline: Where Engineers Are Forged Under Pressure
project.description: A real-time collaborative code interview platform where an AI silently observes the entire coding session and generates a structured candidate evaluation at the end.
project.goal: By Sunday evening, two people join a room, one sets a coding problem with test cases, the other solves it in C/C++/Python/JS with real execution, and both walk away with an AI debrief of the entire session.

concept.one_liner: LeetCode meets Google Docs meets an AI interviewer taking notes.

how_it_differs_from_leetcode.leetcode: Tests output — did the code pass? You're alone. Binary pass/fail.
how_it_differs_from_leetcode.crucible: Evaluates process — how did you get there? Interviewer watches live. AI generates nuanced behavioral analysis.

flow: Interviewer creates a room and configures the problem (description, examples, hidden test cases).
Interviewer shares the room link with the candidate.
Candidate joins, selects their language (C / C++ / Python / JavaScript), and starts coding.
Both see the same code editor in real-time. The candidate writes and runs code against test cases.
Code executes server-side via Judge0 or Piston API. Results appear instantly: passed, failed, TLE, runtime error.
The AI silently records everything: keystrokes, deletions, pauses, run attempts, test case iterations.
When the session ends, both parties receive a detailed AI-generated debrief.

landing_page.description: Clean, minimal. A single 'Create Room' button for the interviewer. No signup, no auth, no friction.

room_setup.description: Interviewer-only screen to configure the problem before sharing the link.
elements: Hidden test cases (candidate sees pass/fail, not the actual test)
'Copy Room Link' button to share with the candidate

room.description: The main coding environment. Split layout, shared between both users in real-time.

left_panel.name: Code Editor
elements: Monaco Editor (the engine behind VS Code)
Language dropdown at the top: C, C++, Python, JavaScript
Every keystroke syncs in real-time between both users via Yjs/WebSocket

right_panel.name: Problem & Results
elements: Top half: Problem statement with examples
Bottom half: Test case results table (input, expected output, actual output, pass/fail status)
'Run Code' button (executes against visible test cases)
'Submit' button (executes against all test cases including hidden ones)
'End Session' button (interviewer only — triggers AI debrief generation)

debrief_page.description: After the session ends, both parties are redirected here. AI-generated evaluation displayed in a clean, printable format. Shareable via a unique URL.

tech_stack.backend: Next.js API Routes + Node.js WebSocket server

code_execution.why_judge0: Free, hosted code execution engine. Handles compilation, sandboxing, execution, timeout enforcement, and memory limits out of the box. Supports 60+ languages. Send code + stdin via API, get stdout + stderr + execution metadata back. No Docker setup needed.

tech_stack.ai_evaluation: Claude API or OpenAI API — single call at session end with full session transcript
tech_stack.state_management: In-memory (JavaScript Map on the server, no database)
tech_stack.auth: None — room links are the access control

data_model.note: No database. Everything lives in a JavaScript Map on the server.

room.roomId: string — unique room identifier (nanoid or UUID)
room.language: string — currently selected language (c | cpp | python | javascript)

timeline.event: keystroke | deletion | run | submit | pause | language_change

room.code: string — current editor state (synced via Yjs)
room.debrief: object | null — AI-generated evaluation, populated after session ends

ai_debrief_spec.description: The AI receives the complete session transcript at session end and generates a structured evaluation.

input_to_ai.problem: The full problem statement and test cases
input_to_ai.timeline: Complete array of timestamped events (keystrokes, runs, pauses, deletions)
input_to_ai.final_code: The last version of the code in the editor
input_to_ai.run_history: All code execution attempts with results
input_to_ai.session_duration: Total time from start to end

approach_analysis: How did the candidate start? (Jumped into code vs. planned first vs. asked clarifying questions)
Did they identify edge cases before or after writing code?
Did they start with brute force and optimize, or aim for optimal from the start?
problem_solving_behavior: Where did they get stuck and how did they recover?
How many times did they hit 'Run' before submitting?
Which test cases failed first and how did they iterate?
Did they switch languages midway? (interesting signal worth noting)
code_quality: Clean code vs. spaghetti under pressure

options: Strong — clear thinking, efficient solution, clean code, good recovery from setbacks
Mixed — got to a solution but with significant struggles or code quality concerns
Weak — fundamental gaps in problem-solving approach or coding ability

tasks: Scaffold Next.js project with Tailwind
Build landing page with 'Create Room' flow
Implement room creation API route (generates roomId, stores in memory)
Build room setup page (problem configuration form)
Set up WebSocket server for room connections
Integrate Monaco Editor into the room view
Set up Yjs with y-monaco for CRDT-based real-time collaboration
Add language selector dropdown (C, C++, Python, JS)
Build the split layout: editor left, problem + results right
Test real-time sync between two browser tabs

sunday_morning.focus: Code execution and test case system
tasks: Integrate Judge0 API for code execution
Build 'Run Code' flow: send code + stdin to Judge0, display results
Implement test case evaluation: run against all cases, show pass/fail table
Add 'Submit' flow with hidden test cases
Handle edge cases: compilation errors, timeouts, runtime errors
Build session timeline recorder (track keystrokes, runs, pauses)
Implement 'End Session' flow: collect timeline + final code + test results
Send session data to Claude/GPT API with the debrief prompt
Build the debrief page: render AI evaluation in a clean, structured layout
Add shareable debrief URL
Polish UI, fix bugs, test end-to-end with a real session

out_of_scope: Video or audio chat between participants
definition_of_done: An interviewer can create a room and configure a coding problem with test cases.
A candidate can join via a shared link and see the same problem.
Both users see a shared Monaco editor with real-time keystroke sync.
The candidate can select C, C++, Python, or JavaScript.
Clicking 'Run' executes the code and shows test case results.
Clicking 'Submit' runs all test cases including hidden ones.
Clicking 'End Session' triggers AI debrief generation.
Both parties see a structured AI evaluation of the candidate's performance.
